name: Covão dos Conchos and Lagoa Comprida Loop
name_original: Rota das 5 Lagoas — percurso circular na Serra da Estrela
description: This difficult 6.9-mile circular hike in Serra da Estrela features 5 lagoons and the unique Covão dos Conchos spillway.
region_intro: Hiking around Lapa Dos Dinheiros offers access to varied terrain within the Serra da Estrela region of Portugal. The landscape features include forested areas, river valleys, and elevated viewpoints, with several lakes and dams contributing to the natural scenery. The region provides a network of paths suitable for those seeking easy hiking trails. Best easy hiking trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros The most popular easy hiking route is Tour da Eira (PR 4) — circular route in Serra da Estrela, a 2.0 miles (3.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the surrounding Serra da Estrela landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop from Serra da Estrela, an easy 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This loop trail explores the area around Covão do Curral Dam and Covão do Ferro Lake. Local hikers also love the Long Lake loop from Albufeira do Covão do Forno, a 2.3 miles (3.6 km) trail leading through a landscape featuring a long lake, often completed in about 1 hour 1 minute. Hiking around Lapa Dos Dinheiros is defined by forested valleys, mountain views, and serene lake environments. The network offers options for various ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails suitable for beginners and families. The routes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Lapa Dos Dinheiros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: There are 14 easy hiking trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros, offering a variety of landscapes from forested areas to river valleys and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy circular hiking routes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Yes, many of the easy trails are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2174795785/tour-da-eira-pr-4-circular-route-in-serra-da-estrela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour da Eira (PR 4)&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 2.0-mile (3.3 km) loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10370014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão do Curral Dam – Covão do Ferro Lake loop&lt;/a&gt; is another easy 3.2-mile (5.1 km) option.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring snow to higher elevations of Serra da Estrela, making some trails more challenging.

Q: Are the easy trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the easy trails are generally suitable for families. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake loop from Albufeira do Covão do Forno&lt;/a&gt;, at 2.3 miles (3.6 km), offer gentle terrain and scenic views that are enjoyable for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the easy hiking trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Most trails in the Serra da Estrela region, including those around Lapa Dos Dinheiros, are dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock, and to carry waste bags.

Q: Are there any waterfalls on the easy hiking routes?
A: Yes, you can find waterfalls on some easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17313329" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caniçada Waterfall loop from Lapa dos Dinheiros&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.3-mile (3.7 km) trail that takes you to the beautiful Caniçada Waterfall. Another notable waterfall nearby is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5904227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço da Broca de Serapitel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Easy hikes in Lapa Dos Dinheiros offer diverse views, including forested valleys, serene lake environments, and panoramic mountain vistas of the Serra da Estrela. Trails often lead to viewpoints overlooking the landscape, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Serra da Estrela loop from Sabugueiro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see near the easy trails?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the trails, you can explore natural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/974890" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lapa dos Dinheiros River Beach&lt;/a&gt; is also a pleasant spot to visit.

Q: What should I bring for an easy hike in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: Even for easy hikes, it's advisable to bring comfortable walking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket as mountain weather can change quickly. A basic first-aid kit is also recommended.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads for easy hikes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in or near the villages and starting points of many trails around Lapa Dos Dinheiros. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages or local signage.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros?
A: The easy trails in Lapa Dos Dinheiros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the peaceful natural surroundings, and the accessibility for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there places to eat or have a coffee after an easy hike?
A: Yes, Lapa Dos Dinheiros and nearby villages offer local cafes and restaurants where you can enjoy a meal or a coffee after your hike. These establishments often provide traditional regional cuisine.
tips:
Located at an altitude of approximately 1,600 meters, Lagoa Comprida is the largest water reservoir in the Serra da Estrela, intended for hydroelectric generation.

This natural lake lies in a wide depression of glacial origin, approximately one kilometer long, which fed the Covão Grande glacier. Numerous glacial scars are visible along its banks, making this lake one of the most interesting geomorphological sites in the Serra da Estrela.

Construction on this lake began in 1911, and by 1914, it was already six meters high. Later, in 1965, it underwent heightening work that increased its height to 28 meters, currently boasting a storage capacity of approximately 14 cubic hectometers, flooding an area of approximately 80 hectares.

Small streams of water flow into this lagoon, as well as water retained in the Covões dos Conchos and Covões do Meio and carried to this lagoon through underground tunnels.

We meet a lot of people on the route, especially on August 16, the trail is easy but many areas of scree require good shoes and flexible ankles. The landscapes are magnificent with the blue sky and the heat is tempered by the altitude. The short swim in the water reservoir of the dam allows you to relax after the walk.

Covão dos Conchos is one of those places that seems to have come from a parallel universe. At first glance, it is a mysterious hole in the middle of the lake, a funnel that seems to swallow the waters in mesmerizing silence. But this phenomenon is not the work of nature, but rather of human engineering: it is a tunnel built in the 1950s to divert the waters of Ribeira das Naves to Lagoa Comprida, the largest reservoir in Serra da Estrela, where it supplies hydroelectric power.

Interestingly, in the driest summers, the water level of the lake can drop so much that the tunnel becomes visible, as if patiently awaiting the next flood.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Tour of the 5 Lagoons?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult hike, demanding good fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and prior alpine experience. It's classified on the Swiss Alpine Club (SAC) Hiking Scale as approximately SAC 4-6, indicating a significant level of challenge. Expect technical, difficult, or potentially hazardous terrain in certain sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses predominantly rocky terrain, featuring granite mounds, pillars, and boulder fields. You'll encounter sections of scree and navigate through glacial valleys. The paths are mostly unpaved, natural, or designated mountain hiking paths.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and scenic features along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers magnificent views across Serra da Estrela's dramatic and wild natural scenery. Key highlights include the various lagoons, with Lagoa Comprida being the largest water reservoir. The most distinctive feature is Covão dos Conchos, an artificial dam with a unique "giant hole" spillway that creates an illusion of a portal. You'll also see glacial scars along the banks of the lagoons and pass through the Covão dos Conchos Trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Tour of the 5 Lagoons?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and high-altitude environment of Serra da Estrela, the best time to hike this route is typically during the late spring, summer, and early autumn months (June to September). During winter, snow and ice can make the trail impassable and significantly more dangerous, requiring specialized equipment and expertise.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to enter the Serra da Estrela Natural Park itself. However, it's always wise to check local regulations before your visit, especially concerning camping or specific activities. Always adhere to Leave No Trace principles to protect the park's natural beauty.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, but access to trails in Serra da Estrela often involves parking near major points like Lagoa Comprida or other designated areas within the park. It's recommended to research specific parking locations closer to your travel date, as conditions can change.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of the 5 Lagoons route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in Serra da Estrela allow dogs, given the difficult and potentially hazardous terrain of this specific route, it might not be suitable for all dogs. If you do bring your dog, ensure they are fit, accustomed to rocky and uneven paths, and always keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and for their safety. Be prepared to carry out all waste.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with official trails. Approximately 27% of the Tour of the 5 Lagoons route covers parts of the [CIMA PR07] Torre * San Romao trail and the [CIMA PR07] Torre * Seia trail.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate layers of clothing for varying weather conditions (even in summer), plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a map and compass (or GPS device), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles are highly recommended for stability on rocky and uneven terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The Serra da Estrela region has some amenities, particularly around more developed areas like Lagoa Comprida, which may offer cafes or basic services. However, this is a wild mountain environment, so do not expect extensive facilities directly at the trailhead. Plan to be self-sufficient for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: Can I find wild swimming spots along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route features several beautiful lagoons, including Long Lake (Lagoa Comprida), specific regulations regarding swimming in these natural reservoirs within Serra da Estrela Natural Park may apply. It's best to check local guidelines or signage at the lagoons themselves to confirm if swimming is permitted and safe.
